content.title: Empty Folder Deleter Utility
content.short_description: Lightweight utility for recursive filesystem cleanup.
content.description: This is a lightweight Python utility designed to clean up file systems by automatically identifying and removing empty directories. The tool addresses the common problem of accumulated empty folders that can clutter directory structures after file operations or reorganizations. It provides a simple command-line interface for users to specify a root directory and efficiently clean up all empty subdirectories recursively.
content.role: I developed this utility from scratch as a personal productivity tool, creating a concise Python script that implements recursive directory traversal to identify and safely remove empty folders. I also compiled the script into an executable (.exe) for easy distribution and use without requiring Python installation. The implementation focuses on simplicity and effectiveness with minimal code footprint.

achievements: Successfully created a functional utility that efficiently cleans empty directories
Reduced manual cleanup time by automating the process of finding and removing empty folders
Provided a portable executable solution that doesn't require Python dependencies
Achieved the core functionality in just 12 lines of clean, readable code
technologies: Python scripting and file system operations
OS module for directory traversal and manipulation

screenshots: Command-line interface showing the folder path input prompt
Output display showing the empty folders being removed
Simple flowchart of the recursive deletion algorithm
